name: Streaming · CS2 tournament overlay platform
description: Rust backend ingesting CS2 Game State Integration, WebSocket fan-out, React control dashboard, browser overlays for OBS, and optional Tauri desktop packaging.
longDescription: End-to-end tournament overlay stack: a Rust service accepts GSI payloads (optional token), maintains authoritative match state, and streams updates over WebSocket to a React dashboard and HTML overlay scenes tuned for OBS Browser Source. The dashboard supports full CRUD for teams and players, manual overrides with audit history, stale detection when GSI stops, JSON import/export, and optional PIN protection on mutating routes. Podman Compose supports development and production-style runs; Tauri can package a desktop build when needed.
features: GSI HTTP ingest with heartbeat/stale detection
Dashboard for match control, assets, theming, and overrides
Multiple overlay layouts (scoreboard, economy, veto, lower thirds, etc.)
Manual override undo stack with audit log
manage.ps1 / Node TUI for developer ergonomics
highlights: Single source of truth feeding both dashboard and overlays
Optional desktop shell via Tauri for on-site use
challenges: Live esports overlays must stay in sync within milliseconds while tolerating game crashes and partial GSI payloads.
solutions: Central Rust state machine, WebSocket delivery, stale indicators, and explicit override tooling with auditability.
security: Do not expose dashboard write APIs to untrusted networks without TLS and access control
